architecture.summary: A survey and categorization framework of three integration patterns between Knowledge Graphs and Large Language Models: (1) KG-enhanced LLMs (KEL), where external structured knowledge augments LLM reasoning and generation; (2) LLM-enhanced KGs (LEK), where LLMs improve KG construction, completion, and reasoning; and (3) collaborative LLMs and KGs (LKC), where both systems interact bidirectionally to solve complex tasks. Information flows between symbolic structured knowledge and neural language models through various integration points.

biblio.title: Practices, opportunities and challenges in the fusion of knowledge graphs and large language models

claims.statement: The fusion of Knowledge Graphs and Large Language Models leverages their complementary strengths to address limitations of both technologies.
KG-enhanced LLMs (KEL) enhance knowledge representation, reasoning, and question answering by integrating external structured knowledge.
LLM-enhanced KGs (LEK) improve knowledge graph construction, completion, and reasoning.
Collaborative LLMs and KGs (LKC) enable bidirectional interaction between both systems to solve complex tasks.
Knowledge acquisition and real-time updates are key challenges in KG-LLM fusion.
